description: Canonical parameter dictionary for inductive components, synthesized from a real-datasheet corpus (Murata, TDK, Wurth Elektronik, Coilcraft, Bourns, Vishay, TAIYO YUDEN, Fair-Rite, Laird, Sunlord) spanning four sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) power inductors (shielded/unshielded drum/molded chokes) which populate inductance, the DC-bias derating curve (inductance_vs_dc_current), saturation_current and rms_current as DISTINCT ratings, dc_resistance, self_resonant_frequency, and temperature_rise_current; (2) rf_multilayer / wirewound RF chip inductors which populate inductance, quality_factor and self_resonant_frequency prominently, ac_resistance, and rated_current; (3) ferrite_bead (chip beads) which populate impedance |Z| (typically specced at 100 MHz) plus its resistance_component and reactance_component split, rated_current, and dc_resistance - a bead is inductive below SRF and resistive (dissipative) above it, which is the whole point of the part; (4) common_mode_choke (CMC, data-line and power-line) which populate common_mode_impedance, inductance (per-winding, common-mode), rated_voltage, withstanding_voltage (hipot), and insulation_resistance between windings. A part's sub_type selects which optional keys apply. The critical modeling rule this dictionary enforces: saturation_current (a MAGNETIC limit, the DC bias at which |L| drops a stated percentage - e.g. -30%) and rms_current (a THERMAL limit, the DC current for a stated self-heating temperature rise - e.g. +40 degC) are SEPARATE keys and must never be conflated; the usable continuous current is the lower of the two. inductance is only meaningful with its measurement frequency, so put the test frequency (F) in every inductance measurement's conditions. Base-SI throughout: inductance in H (10 uH is 0.00001), no SI prefixes anywhere; the dimensionless quality factor Q uses V/V; impedance, DCR, and the bead R/X split use ohm. component.polarity is not meaningful for a passive inductor. Condition axes for this family: F (test/spot frequency, for inductance/Q/impedance/SRF/AC resistance), T_A (ambient), I_DC (DC bias current, for the saturation-derating and temp-rise curves), T_RISE (self-heating temperature rise, for the rms current rating point), L_DROP (note-only inductance-drop percentage at which a saturation current is defined). Pinout: a 2-terminal power or RF inductor and a ferrite bead have functions A and B (non-polarized, interchangeable). A common-mode choke is a 4-terminal part with functions L1_IN, L1_OUT, L2_IN, L2_OUT - the two windings are L1 (L1_IN->L1_OUT) and L2 (L2_IN->L2_OUT), and the phasing dot convention is that the *_IN pins are the dotted (same-phase) ends; preserve winding identity so a consumer wires the two lines in the correct sense. Saturation-derating and temperature-rise curves and full impedance-vs-frequency sweeps are graph-derived - capture the tabulated scalar points and the stated curve rows. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Inductance (nominal)
parameters.hint: Nominal inductance in henries (base-SI; 10 uH is 0.00001, 100 nH is 0.0000001). Only meaningful with its test frequency - put F in the conditions of every measurement (a 100 kHz power-inductor L and a 100 MHz RF-chip L are different measurements). For a common-mode choke this is the per-winding common-mode inductance.

parameters.name: Inductance tolerance
parameters.hint: Inductance tolerance as a percentage (e.g. +/-20%). Printed tolerance letter codes map here: M=+/-20%, K=+/-10%, J=+/-5%, G=+/-2%, F=+/-1%, S/N=+/-30%.

parameters.name: Inductance vs DC bias current (derating curve)
parameters.hint: The inductance-vs-DC-bias derating curve: one measurement per DC-bias point (array over I_DC), each the effective inductance at that superimposed DC current and a stated test frequency. Ferromagnetic cores lose inductance as bias rises toward saturation; this curve is how a consumer picks a working current below meaningful roll-off. Hold F in the conditions.

parameters.name: Saturation current
parameters.hint: A MAGNETIC limit: the DC bias current at which the inductance has dropped by a STATED percentage from its zero-bias value (e.g. -10%, -20%, -30%) - carry the drop percentage on the L_DROP axis (note-only) and/or in conditionsVerbatim. A datasheet often lists several Isat rows at different drop criteria; emit one measurement per criterion. NEVER conflate with rms_current (a thermal limit) - the usable current is the lower of the two.

parameters.name: RMS (heating) current
parameters.hint: A THERMAL limit: the DC current that produces a STATED self-heating temperature rise (e.g. +20 degC, +40 degC) - carry the rise on the T_RISE axis. A datasheet often lists rows at several rise criteria; emit one measurement per criterion. NEVER conflate with saturation_current (a magnetic limit); the usable continuous current is the lower of Isat and Irms.

parameters.name: Rated (maximum continuous) current
parameters.hint: Maximum continuous / rated current where a datasheet gives a single combined figure (common on RF chip inductors and ferrite beads) rather than splitting into distinct saturation and heating limits. May be derated over ambient temperature (array over T_A). When both a magnetic and a thermal limit are separately tabulated, use saturation_current and rms_current instead.

parameters.name: DC resistance (DCR)
parameters.hint: DC winding resistance in ohms (base-SI; 100 mohm is 0.1). Typically tabulated as a typ and a max. On a common-mode choke this is the per-line (per-winding) DCR.

parameters.name: AC resistance (ACR)
parameters.hint: AC resistance at a stated frequency (array over F) - the effective series resistance including skin and proximity effects and core loss; rises with frequency well above the DCR. Common on RF chip inductors.

parameters.name: Self-resonant frequency (SRF)
parameters.hint: The frequency at which the inductor's parasitic capacitance resonates with its inductance and net reactance passes through zero; above SRF the part behaves capacitively. Usually a typ min. On a ferrite bead, SRF marks the transition from inductive to resistive behavior.

parameters.name: Quality factor (Q)
parameters.hint: Quality factor Q = reactance/AC-resistance at a stated frequency (dimensionless, unit V/V); higher is lower-loss. Array over F - the datasheet gives Q at one or more spot frequencies, often a guaranteed Qmin. Prominent on RF chip inductors.

parameters.name: Impedance magnitude |Z|
parameters.hint: Ferrite bead: the impedance magnitude |Z| at a stated frequency (array over F) - the headline rating, almost always specified at 100 MHz (the |Z|@100MHz alias). The bead's job is to present this impedance to high-frequency noise; total |Z| = sqrt(R^2 + X^2) of resistance_component and reactance_component.

parameters.name: Impedance resistance component (Rs)
parameters.hint: Ferrite bead: the resistive (dissipative, real) part of the complex impedance at a stated frequency (array over F). Above SRF the bead is dominantly resistive - this component is what actually absorbs and dissipates noise energy as heat rather than reflecting it.

parameters.name: Impedance reactance component (Xs)
parameters.hint: Ferrite bead: the reactive (inductive, imaginary) part of the complex impedance at a stated frequency (array over F). Dominates below SRF; at low frequency a bead is essentially a small inductor whose reactance rises with frequency.

parameters.name: Common-mode impedance (Zcm)
parameters.hint: Common-mode choke: the common-mode impedance presented to common-mode (in-phase) currents at a stated frequency (array over F). The CMC's function is to block common-mode noise while passing the differential (signal/power) current with low impedance.

parameters.name: Rated voltage
parameters.hint: Common-mode choke (and some power inductors): the maximum rated working (DC) voltage across the part (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Withstanding (hipot) voltage
parameters.hint: Common-mode choke: the dielectric withstanding (hi-pot) voltage the isolation between windings survives for a stated time without breakdown - a safety/isolation rating, not an operating voltage.

parameters.name: Insulation resistance
parameters.hint: Common-mode choke: the insulation (isolation) resistance between the two windings at a stated test voltage - very large (megohms/gigohms; base-SI, so 100 Mohm is 100000000).

parameters.name: Temperature-rise current curve
parameters.hint: The self-heating (temperature-rise vs DC current) characteristic: one measurement per DC-bias point (array over I_DC), each carrying the resulting self-heating temperature rise in conditionsVerbatim. The tabulated form of the thermal-derating graph from which rms_current's stated-rise points are read.

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: The operating (ambient) temperature range (min/max), including self-heating on power parts (e.g. -40 to +125 degC).
